post.description: Creates a new data ingestion job to pull data into your Concert instance from a third-party tool or service. Connect with code-hosting platforms (GitHub) to pull in source code repositories, container registries (AWS ECR, AWS S3, etc.) to pull in images and other build artifacts, and deployment repositories. The ingested components appear in your Inventory and can use these ingested components to define your applications and environments.

post.description: Retrieves all unprocessed ('landing zone') data ingested by your Concert instance based on the search criteria. The request body must include at least one parameter by which to filter search results. This search request applies an 'AND' logic to filter inputs. The 'table of contents' is like an index of ingested data irrespective of the route in which data is brought in. It also helps facilitate backup and archive processes. Concert applies the this concept through landing zone (lz) and evidence store (el). Unprocessed data is placed in the landing zone and processed data is placed in the evidence store.

parameters.name: InstanceId

post.description:
This request accepts a SearchPayload object that defines how event data
should be filtered and sorted. The default sort is based on processing_end_time in desc order.

Supported filtering operators:
  - NOT IN, IN, =, !=, RANGE, LIKE

Supported filtering fields:
  - date_range, event_duration, lz_path, data_type, event_type,
    event_method, status, ingested_by, event_id, file_name, metadata
  - Metadata filters must use dot notation in the format: metadata.KEY, an example: metadata.parent_event_id
  - multiple filtering fields seperated by commas are supported 
    for the operator LIKE

Supported sorting fields:
  - event_id, data_source, status, processing_start_time, processing_end_time,
    file_name, data_type, event_method, event_type, file_size, ingested_by, timestamp

    data_type, el_path, submitted_by, evidence_id, file_name, metadata

post.description:
- An assessment will be created based on the request made within a given assessment period.
- There will be only one assessment within the timeframe of one period, which will be created by the first request made in that period.
- Metric input with the request is stored into the DB with timestamp.
- Latest of the values for the metrics available in the period will be used for evaluation/assessment, irrespective of data input along with the request.
- Any further requests for evaluation will update the existing assessment in the period.
- Name and assessment period will be considered only when posture or assessment is created by the first request in the period. 
- Updates to an existing assessment will not change assessment name or assessement period.
- To enable actions to be created when assessments are done, define the cluster and its relation to the app in Concert or pass target_component_type and target_component_identifier in the custom_metadata section in /evaluate payload.

requestBody.description:
## How to Use
- The .tar or .zip file should be created on a Linux / Mac machine, as extraction happens in the grader container, which is Linux-based.
  ### Example for creation of Archives(.tar / .zip files)
  Navigate to the domain-specific folder that contains all related files, and run the zip or tar command within that folder to archive its contents correctly.
  ```bash
  zip -r <file_name>.zip .
  ```
  ```bash
  tar -cvf <file_name>.tar .
  ```
- Supported file types for import: .tar, .tar.gz, and .zip. Files must not be password protected.
- Zipping or tarring an entire folder containing domain files (e.g., library, profile, criteria) is not supported.
- Only the necessary domain files (library, profile, criteria, etc.) should be included in the .tar or .zip archive.
- The prefixes used in the profile.json, library.json, and criteria.yaml files must be consistent across all related files. For example, if the profile file is named java_runtime_profile.json, then the corresponding files should follow the same prefix pattern: java_runtime_library.json and java_runtime_criteria.yaml
- When a language file exists, include the externals_ prefix in its name (e.g., external_fr for the French language file).
- Nested archives are not allowed due to security concerns (zip bombs/tar bombs). Archives with excessive compression ratios (>100:1), too many files (>1,000), or extracted sizes exceeding 1GB will be rejected.
- The file must be named exactly input_data_keys.json, with no prefixes or suffixes. Files such as prefix_input_data_keys.json or input_data_keys_suffix.json will be rejected.
- To add specific libraries, profiles, or postures to an existing domain, use the POST operation. The /import endpoint allows adding libraries to existing domains with limited validations.
- A category file must be provided when importing a domain that supports categories.
- Upload a ZIP file containing the library, profile, requirements, and input data dictionary required for evaluation.
- Out-of-Box resources will not be updated even for successful import. Only additional resources will be imported.
- Note: The title is changed from Import domain to Import library for external API. In upcoming versions we need to seggregate these APIs

IngestionjobAutomationPrototype.description:
The automation registration object. A combinations of fields are supposed to be used together. The following are the sample payload combinations:
- Name, description, use_case, status, action, component_type and component_id are common parameters.
- action == Open GitHub issue/Open Jira issue/ Open ServiceNow ticket/Open SalesForce ticket then target_service_details is present.
- When use_case == CVE tracking, the Option object can have priority, custom_score, risk_score, and cvss_score.
- When use_case == Compliance assessment, the Option object can have profile_name and result.
- When use_case == Certificate expiry, the Option object will have only expires_in.
- When use_case == sca, the Option object can have action field with values review, replace, upgrade.
- use_case == Compliance assessment/Certificate expiry then component_type will only be environment.

assessment_period.description:
- Valid assessment periods are "hour", "day", "week", "month", "quarter", "latest".
- All the periods are based on UTC
  - Hour - A one-hour period from the top of an hour to the end of an hour, for example from 1:00 PM to 2:00 PM
  - Day - A 24-hour period starting at 12:00 AM (midnight) of one day and ending at 12:00 AM (midnight) of the following day
  - Week - A 7-day period starting at 12:00AM(midnight) of Monday and ending at 12:00AM(midnight) of following Monday
  - Month - A one-month period starting at 12:00 AM (midnight) of the first day of the month and ending at 12:00 AM (midnight) of the day of the following month
  - Quarter - A three-month period starting at 12:00 AM (midnight) of the first day of January, April, July, or October and ending at 12:00 AM (midnight) of the first day of the first month of the following quarter
  - Latest - The time period from the first recorded timestamp to latest
- For 'latest', each request will create a new assessment using the latest metric values available in the entire timeframe.
